response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced senior full-stack developer with strong craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD) and genuine mentoring credentials, but he does not meet the core minimum qualifications for this role. The position requires 8 years of C++ experience; his C++ exposure is limited to a ~6-month stint at National Instruments and a thesis project. His systems-level work (low-latency services at Dunnhumby) was in DLang, and his background centers on web/product engineering rather than building or leading a large-scale cloud compute platform. The gap on the hard requirements is too large to justify an interview for this specific role.
strengths: Some high-throughput distributed-systems experience (low-latency microservices handling millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
Strong engineering-practice advocacy: TDD, trunk-based development, code katas, and multi-day courses/workshops demonstrating mentoring ability
Broad polyglot experience across backend, infrastructure tooling, and DevOps (Docker, Kubernetes, CI servers)
concerns: Falls far short of the 8-year C++ minimum — only ~6 months of professional C++ work
No production cloud architecture or compute-platform ownership; experience is mostly product/web engineering
No formal technical-leadership role leading cross-team strategy for infrastructure at scale

key_factors.factor: Lack of cloud compute platform experience
